preamble.summary: Famine strikes. Jacob sends his sons to Egypt for grain. Joseph recognizes them, accuses them of spying, imprisons Simeon, demands Benjamin.
preamble.remarkable: Jubilees follows Genesis 42 closely but adds jubilee dating. The emotional core — Joseph's hidden tears — is preserved.
preamble.friction: The dialogue is compressed compared to Genesis 42.

verses.text_reference: And the famine began to come into the land.
verses.rendering: In the first year of the third week of the forty-fifth jubilee, the famine spread across the land. The rain refused to fall.

verses.text_reference: And in Egypt there was food, for Joseph had stored it.
verses.rendering: The earth became barren, but in Egypt there was food, for Joseph had stored grain during the seven years of plenty.

verses.text_reference: And all lands came to Egypt to buy.
verses.rendering: The Egyptians came to Joseph for food. He opened the storehouses. People from every land came to buy.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob sent his ten sons but kept Benjamin.
verses.rendering: Jacob sent his ten sons to Egypt for grain, but he did not send Benjamin.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph recognized them, but they did not recognize him.
verses.rendering: They went and arrived in Egypt. Joseph saw them and recognized them, but they did not recognize him.

verses.text_reference: And he spoke roughly and accused them of being spies.
verses.rendering: He spoke harshly and asked: Where have you come from? They said: From Canaan, to buy food. He said: You are spies!

verses.text_reference: And they said: 'We are twelve brethren. The youngest is with our father.'
verses.rendering: They protested: We are twelve brothers, sons of one father. The youngest is with our father, and one is no more.

verses.text_reference: And he put them in ward three days, then demanded Benjamin.
verses.rendering: He put them in custody for three days. On the third day he said: Let one brother remain while the rest carry grain home.

verses.text_reference: 'Bring me your youngest brother.'
verses.rendering: Then bring your youngest brother to me, so I will know you are not spies. They agreed.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph heard them say: 'We are guilty concerning our brother.'
verses.rendering: Joseph overheard them say: We are truly guilty concerning our brother. We saw his anguish when he pleaded with us. Joseph turned away and wept in secret.

verses.text_reference: And he kept Simeon and sent the nine away with grain.
verses.rendering: He kept Simeon alone and sent the other nine with grain and their money hidden in their sacks.

verses.text_reference: And they told Jacob, and he refused to send Benjamin.
verses.rendering: They told Jacob everything. He refused to send Benjamin: You have robbed me of my children!

verses.text_reference: And Jacob said: 'Joseph is not, Simeon is not, and ye will take Benjamin.'
verses.rendering: Jacob said: Joseph is gone, Simeon is gone, and now you want Benjamin. Everything falls on me!

verses.text_reference: And when the food was consumed, Jacob said: 'Go again.'
verses.rendering: When the food ran out, Jacob said: Go back and buy more grain.

verses.text_reference: And Judah said: 'The man will not see us without Benjamin.'
verses.rendering: Judah spoke up: The man warned us — you will not see my face unless your brother is with you.

verses.text_reference: And Israel said: 'Why did ye tell the man ye had another brother?'
verses.rendering: Israel said: Why did you treat me so badly by telling the man you had another brother?

verses.text_reference: And Judah said: 'I will be surety for him.'
verses.rendering: Judah said: I myself will guarantee his safety. If I do not bring him back, let me bear the blame forever.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob sent Benjamin with them.
verses.rendering: Jacob sent his son Benjamin with them. Judah stood as surety.

verses.text_reference: And they stood before Joseph, and he saw Benjamin.
verses.rendering: When they stood before Joseph and he saw Benjamin, he was overcome with emotion.

verses.text_reference: And he made them a feast.
verses.rendering: He prepared a feast for them. They ate, drank, and were glad.

verses.text_reference: And Benjamin's portion was seven times more.
verses.rendering: Benjamin's portion was seven times larger than anyone else's.
translator_notes: 'Seven times' amplifies Genesis 43:34's 'five times.' Seven is Jubilees' signature number.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph placed his cup in Benjamin's sack.
verses.rendering: Joseph devised a test. He had his silver cup placed in Benjamin's sack.

verses.text_reference: And his servants found the cup in Benjamin's sack.
verses.rendering: They departed in the morning, but Joseph sent servants after them. The cup was found in Benjamin's sack.

verses.text_reference: And they were brought back before Joseph.
verses.rendering: They were brought back. They fell before him. Judah said: God has uncovered our guilt. We are all your slaves.

verses.text_reference: And Joseph said: 'Only the one with the cup shall be my slave.'
verses.rendering: Joseph said: Only the man in whose possession the cup was found will be my slave. The rest — go in peace to your father.
